title: Joby Aviation Reports Q1 2026 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
text: Joby Aviation reported Q1 2026 earnings with a net loss of $110 million, representing a $12 million improvement from Q4's $122 million loss. The company maintained a robust financial position with approximately $2.5 billion in cash and equivalents. Cash usage for the quarter totaled $195 million, excluding capital raises. The company invested $78 million in property and equipment, including a $62 million purchase of a new Ohio manufacturing facility. The company successfully completed its SR3 audit with the FAA, a critical step toward final certification. Additionally, Joby executed full transition flights with its turbine-electric VTOL aircraft, demonstrating maneuverability and endurance to the US army. Recent demonstrations in New York and San Francisco showcased the aircraft's capabilities. Manufacturing operations are ramping significantly in Ohio with propeller blade production underway. The company has parts in production for nine aircraft and five dedicated for FAA testing. Management expressed confidence about commencing passenger operations before year-end through partnerships in Dubai and domestic EIPP markets.
